question: [File: 'src/periphery/TakeAmountsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: buyerAssetsToUnits / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a buy offer (offer.buy=true, maxAssets>0) at any tick, call consumableUnits then take with the returned units, such that mulDivDown(mulDivUp(remaining, WAD, buyerPrice), buyerPrice, WAD) < remaining causes consumed[maker][group] to increase by strictly less than the remaining budget, leaving the offer re-takeable and allowing the group budget to be exceeded across sequential fills? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, offer.maxAssets>0, consumed[maker][group] = C where 0 < C < maxAssets, buyerPrice = sellerPrice + settlementFee, buyerPrice <= WAD. Call sequence: (1) consumableUnits returns units = mulDivUp(maxAssets-C, WAD, buyerPrice); (2) take(offer, ..., units, ...) executes, computing buyerAssets = mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice, WAD) <= maxAssets-C; (3) consumed increases by buyerAssets < maxAssets-C; (4) offer is not fully consumed; (5) repeat. Invariant tested: consumed[maker][group] must reach maxAssets after consumableUnits units are taken. Scoped impact: offer overfilled / reused. Proof idea: fuzz (tick, settlementFee, C, maxAssets) and assert consumed[maker][group] == maxAssets after one consumableUnits-guided take.

[File: 'src/periphery/TakeAmountsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: sellerAssetsToUnits / ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a sell offer (offer.buy=false, maxAssets>0), call consumableUnits which returns mulDivDown(remaining, WAD, sellerPrice) units, then have take revert with ConsumedAssets because mulDivUp(mulDivDown(remaining, WAD, sellerPrice), sellerPrice, WAD) > remaining, griefing the bundler and preventing the offer from being filled? Preconditions: offer.buy=false, offer.maxAssets>0, consumed[maker][group] = C, remaining = maxAssets-C > 0, sellerPrice = offerPrice (no fee subtracted for sell offer), tick chosen so mulDivUp(mulDivDown(remaining, WAD, sellerPrice), sellerPrice, WAD) > remaining. Call sequence: (1) consumableUnits returns units = mulDivDown(remaining, WAD, sellerPrice); (2) take computes sellerAssets = mulDivUp(units, sellerPrice, WAD) > remaining; (3) consumed[maker][group] + sellerAssets > maxAssets; (4) require(newConsumed <= maxAssets) reverts. Invariant tested: consumableUnits must return units that do not cause take to revert with ConsumedAssets. Scoped impact: offer griefed / fund freeze. Proof idea: fuzz (tick, remaining) and assert take(consumableUnits units) does not revert with ConsumedAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/TakeAmountsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: buyerAssetsToUnits / sellerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the settlement fee staleness between the TakeAmountsLib view call and the actual Midnight.take execution, such that a fee increase between the two calls causes the units returned by buyerAssetsToUnits to produce a different buyerAssets in take than expected, allowing the offer to be overfilled or the taker to be undercharged? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, maxAssets>0, feeSetter changes settlementFee between consumableUnits call and take. Call sequence: (1) consumableUnits reads old settlementFee, computes units = mulDivUp(remaining, WAD, oldBuyerPrice); (2) feeSetter increases settlementFee; (3) take uses new settlementFee, computes newBuyerPrice > oldBuyerPrice, buyerAssets = mulDivDown(units, newBuyerPrice, WAD) > remaining; (4) consumed exceeds maxAssets, take reverts. Invariant tested: consumed[maker][group] must not exceed maxAssets regardless of fee changes between view and execute. Scoped impact: offer griefed / accounting corruption. Proof idea: unit test with fee change between consumableUnits and take, assert no revert and correct consumed.

[File: 'src/periphery/TakeAmountsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: sellerAssetsToUnits / ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged taker exploit a settlement fee decrease between the consumableUnits view call and the actual take, such that for a sell offer (offer.buy=false, maxAssets>0), the units returned by sellerAssetsToUnits produce sellerAssets < remaining in take, leaving the offer under-consumed and re-takeable? Preconditions: offer.buy=false, maxAssets>0, feeSetter decreases settlementFee between view and execute. Call sequence: (1) consumableUnitsLib reads old fee, computes units = mulDivDown(remaining, WAD, oldSellerPrice); (2) feeSetter decreases fee; (3) take uses new sellerPrice (unchanged for sell offer, sellerPrice = offerPrice), so sellerAssets = mulDivUp(units, offerPrice, WAD) may differ; (4) consumed < maxAssets; (5) offer re-takeable. Invariant tested: offer must be fully consumed after consumableUnits-guided take. Scoped impact: offer reused. Proof idea: differential test with and without fee change, assert consumed == maxAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/TakeAmountsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits / MidnightBundles] Can an unprivileged taker, using MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget with a sell offer (offer.buy=true, maxAssets>0) that is partially consumed, cause the bundler to compute unitsToTake = min(sellerAssetsToUnits(...), takes[i].units, consumableUnits(...)) where consumableUnits returns units that, when taken, produce sellerAssets > remaining due to mulDivUp rounding, causing the inner take to revert and the bundler to silently skip the offer, resulting in OutOfOffers revert? Invariant tested: bundler must fill target assets when sufficient offer capacity exists. Scoped impact: fund freeze / offer griefed. Proof idea: fuzz (C, maxAssets, tick) and assert bundler succeeds when capacity >= target.

[File: 'src/periphery/TakeAmountsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: buyerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged taker call buyerAssetsToUnits with a buy offer (offer.buy=true) where offerPrice < settlementFee, causing an arithmetic underflow in sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee (Solidity 0.8 reverts), while the same call to take would also revert, but the revert in buyerAssetsToUnits propagates through ConsumableUnitsLib and causes MidnightBundles to revert entirely (not just skip the offer), freezing the bundler for all subsequent offers in the array? [File: 'src/periphery/TakeAmountsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: sellerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged taker call sellerAssetsToUnits with a buy offer (offer.buy=true) where sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee = 0 (when offerPrice == settlementFee), causing a division by zero in mulDivUp(target, WAD, 0), reverting the call and propagating through ConsumableUnitsLib to revert the bundler entirely? [File: 'src/periphery/TakeAmountsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the race condition where consumed[maker][group] is read in consumableUnits (view) and then increases before take executes, causing the units returned by consumableUnits to exceed the actual remaining capacity, resulting in take reverting with ConsumedUnits or ConsumedAssets and the bundler silently skipping the offer? Preconditions: offer.maxUnits>0, two concurrent takers, consumed[maker][group] = C, maxUnits = C + 10. Call sequence: (1) taker A calls consumableUnits, gets 10 units; (2) taker B calls take(5 units) first, consumed becomes C+5; (3) taker A calls take(10 units), consumed would become C+15 > maxUnits; (4) take reverts; (5) bundler skips. Proof idea: stateful fuzz with two concurrent takers, assert bundler fills target or reverts with OutOfOffers.

[File: 'src/periphery/TakeAmountsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: buyerAssetsToUnits / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged taker, for a buy offer (offer.buy=true, maxAssets>0) at a tick where buyerPrice is close to WAD, repeatedly call take with units = buyerAssetsToUnits(1 wei) such that each take adds 0 to consumed (mulDivDown(1, buyerPrice, WAD) = 0 when buyerPrice < WAD), allowing unlimited units to be taken without consuming the offer? Call sequence: (1) buyerAssetsToUnits(1 wei) returns 1 unit; (2) take(1 unit) computes buyerAssets = mulDivDown(1, buyerPrice, WAD) = 0; (3) consumed += 0; (4) repeat indefinitely. [File: 'src/periphery/TakeAmountsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: Midnight.take / consumed tracking] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the documented edge case 'It is possible to give units to a fully consumed assets-based buy offer with price < 1' to take units from a fully consumed buy offer (consumed == maxAssets) without increasing consumed, effectively reusing the offer after it should be exhausted?
